trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime dual-write strategy clearly designed with Strangler Fig pattern. (2) Phases clearly defined — PostgreSQL schema setup, backfill of 8M records, dual-write with MongoDB primary, reconciliation gate, read cutover to PostgreSQL, MongoDB write disable, decommission with 2-week read-only safety net. (3) Rollback plan via feature flags enabling instant phase reversion; MongoDB kept read-only post-cutover as rollback insurance. (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency risk explicitly identified — dry-run Schema Translator against all 8M documents before Phase 2, with logging of failed translations. (5) Trade-offs include dual-write latency (~5-15ms per secondary write) and operational overhead of maintaining two stores.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough migration architecture with 8 well-defined components including DLQ, Reconciliation Engine, and Schema Translator
Backfill risk handled with timestamp-guarded upserts (WHERE orders.updated_at < EXCLUDED.updated_at) to prevent overwriting newer dual-written data
Shadow read verification pattern adds confidence gradient before read cutover — start at 1%, ramp to 100%
Rigidity points clearly articulate non-negotiable invariants like write ordering and reconciliation gate
MongoDB data shape risk addressed both as a risk item and in implementation guidance (dry-run translation of all 8M docs before Phase 2)
Concrete pitfalls-to-avoid section prevents common migration mistakes (auto-increment IDs, schema changes during dual-write, ObjectId ordering assumptions)
Trade-off analysis compares dual-write vs CDC vs Change Streams with clear rationale for choosing dual-write at this scale
